title: sphaeralcea ambigua var. rugosa
intro: Sphaeralcea ambigua var. rugosa, also known as the Desert Marigold or Rough-leaf Desert Marigold, is a variety of flowering plant that belongs to the marigold family and is native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Sphaeralcea ambigua var. rugosa include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight infection, and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and bronchitis. Sphaeralcea ambigua var. rugosa can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and it is often combined with other herbs in herbal remedies to enhance its medicinal properties.
